entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: The most important raw material for crop improvement programs, currently facing possible extinction

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT PROGRAM
entities.description: Programs designed to improve crop characteristics to avert food shortages and famines

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop whose origin and early domestication occurred in Africa and Asia

entities.name: NORTHEAST QUADRANT OF AFRICA
entities.description: The region where the origin and early domestication of sorghum most probably took place

entities.name: ETHIOPIA-SUDAN BORDER AREA
entities.description: A specific area in the northeast quadrant of Africa associated with sorghum domestication

entities.name: CHAD
entities.description: A country in Africa to which the sorghum domestication area extends westward

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: An important center of diversity for sorghum landraces

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: An important center of diversity for sorghum landraces

entities.name: LANDRACES
entities.description: Original crop varieties that exist in centers of diversity but are endangered

entities.name: FOOD SHORTAGES
entities.description: A state of insufficient food supply that crop improvement programs aim to prevent

entities.name: FAMINES
entities.description: Extreme scarcity of food resulting from food shortages

entities.name: COLLECTION AND CONSERVATION
entities.description: The process of gathering and preserving vanishing germplasm to support crop improvement
